mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easing discount rates and reduced equity risk premia, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month when inflation is not accelerating. This effect is strongest after a sequence of rate cuts and fails if inflation remains elevated or the market has already fully priced the easing path.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a falling FEDFUNDS trend and stable CPIAUCSL, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above recent trend indicates weakening household income and lower future earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is invalid when unemployment rises alongside strong payroll growth, because that combination usually reflects survey noise rather than real labor deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal loses predictive power whenever PAYEMS growth is nonnegative.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS captures stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which should support equity prices over the following month through better sales expectations and operating leverage. The effect fails if sales growth is purely inflation-driven without real demand improvement.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS does not correspond to positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal disappears after controlling for CPIAUCSL acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in INDPRO combined with weak HOUST implies improving industrial supply but persistent housing weakness, a mix that usually supports cyclicals less than defensives and presses the broad index lower over a 5-day horizon. The signal is strongest when production improves faster than construction activity and fails when both series rise together.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises faster than HOUST by at least 1 month trend
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-HOUST pattern is followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered cases, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already near the zero lower bound, further low-rate conditions mainly reinforce liquidity support and reduce discount rates, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails once inflation re-accelerates or the market starts pricing imminent tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if low-FEDFUNDS periods do not deliver above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if returns turn negative whenever CPIAUCSL trend exceeds its prior 6-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains in PAYEMS combined with falling UNRATE indicate genuine labor-market improvement, which should raise expected household income and support equities over the next month. The effect is falsified if payroll growth is accompanied by rising unemployment or if the payroll gains do not translate into positive index returns.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-minus-UNRATE improvement pattern yields negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the interaction adds no incremental predictive value over single-series trends.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after prior weakness signals a housing-led demand recovery that typically feeds into consumer confidence, durable goods demand, and broader equity earnings expectations over a quarter. The signal fails when housing starts rise only because of temporary policy effects while sales and employment remain weak.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start upturns are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns at a rate above 50%, or if the effect vanishes when RSAFS is contracting.

mechanism_narrative: If INDPRO and PAYEMS both accelerate while CPIAUCSL stays contained, the economy is expanding without forcing restrictive price pressure, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days. This mechanism fails when the same growth pattern coincides with rising inflation, because higher rates then dominate the earnings impulse.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint growth-low-inflation condition does not outperform unconditional 60-day SPX returns, or if CPIAUCSL acceleration removes the positive effect entirely.
